Skip to contents
Experimental software—use at your own risk. Results and APIs may be incorrect or change. Users are responsible for checking their data, design, model specification, convergence, identifiability, diagnostics, and interpretation. Important analyses should be independently refitted and cross-checked with bayesTLS (source repository). Agreement is a cross-check, not proof of correctness; shared data or model errors can make both packages agree.
freqTLS 0.2.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference
    • Get started
    • Model details
    • The model and its parameterisation
    • Examples
    • Case 1: zebrafish heat tolerance under normoxia and hyperoxia
    • Case 2: cereal-aphid thermal tolerance
    • Case 3: Snow-gum PSII under Dark and Light recovery
    • Case 4: Drosophila suzukii mortality
    • Case 4.2: Drosophila suzukii awake/coma response
    • Canonical empirical examples: what can and cannot be compared
    • Extensions
    • Cross-checking freqTLS with bayesTLS
    • Frequentist and Bayesian thermal-load sensitivity: strengths, weaknesses, and the blurry line
    • Experimental extension: deterministic heat-injury prediction
    • Profile-likelihood intervals
    • Random effects: hierarchical thermal tolerance
    • More articles...
  • Changelog

Authors and Citation

Authors

  • Shinichi Nakagawa. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

  • Pieter A. Arnold. Author.
    co-author of the bayesTLS framework

  • Patrice Pottier. Author.
    co-author of the bayesTLS framework

  • Daniel W. A. Noble. Author.
    senior author of the bayesTLS thermal-load-sensitivity framework

Citation

Source: inst/CITATION

Nakagawa S, Arnold P, Pottier P, Noble D (2026). freqTLS: Frequentist Inference for Thermal Load Sensitivity Models. R package version 0.2.0.9000, https://github.com/itchyshin/freqTLS. 

@Manual{,
  title = {freqTLS: Frequentist Inference for Thermal Load Sensitivity Models},
  author = {Shinichi Nakagawa and Pieter A. Arnold and Patrice Pottier and Daniel W. A. Noble},
  year = {2026},
  note = {R package version 0.2.0.9000},
  url = {https://github.com/itchyshin/freqTLS},
}

Noble D, Arnold P, Pottier P (2026). bayesTLS: Bayesian Thermal Load Sensitivity Models. R package; the thermal-load-sensitivity framework that freqTLS implements (manuscript in preparation). The brown-shrimp and life-stage zebrafish data were obtained from the CC BY 4.0 bayesTLS distribution; please cite bayesTLS when using them. Other datasets retain their component-specific licences and citations; see the entries below and inst/COPYRIGHTS in the source package., https://github.com/daniel1noble/bayesTLS. 

@Manual{,
  title = {bayesTLS: Bayesian Thermal Load Sensitivity Models},
  author = {Daniel W. A. Noble and Pieter A. Arnold and Patrice Pottier},
  year = {2026},
  note = {R package; the thermal-load-sensitivity framework that freqTLS implements (manuscript in preparation). The brown-shrimp and life-stage zebrafish data were obtained from the CC BY 4.0 bayesTLS distribution; please cite bayesTLS when using them. Other datasets retain their component-specific licences and citations; see the entries below and inst/COPYRIGHTS in the source package.},
  url = {https://github.com/daniel1noble/bayesTLS},
}

Orsted M, Willot Q, Olsen A, Kongsgaard V, Overgaard J (2024). “Data for: Thermal Limits of Survival and Reproduction Depend on Stress Duration: A Case Study of Drosophila suzukii.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.10602268. Dataset, CC BY 4.0. 

@Misc{,
  title = {Data for: Thermal Limits of Survival and Reproduction Depend on Stress Duration: A Case Study of Drosophila suzukii},
  author = {Michael Orsted and Quentin Willot and Andreas Kirk Olsen and Viktor Kongsgaard and Johannes Overgaard},
  year = {2024},
  doi = {10.5281/zenodo.10602268},
  note = {Dataset, CC BY 4.0},
}

Li YJ, Chen SY, Jorgensen LB, Overgaard J, Renault D, Colinet H, Ma CS (2023). “Data for: Interspecific Differences in Thermal Tolerance Landscape Explain Aphid Community Abundance under Climate Change.” doi:10.5061/dryad.mcvdnck4j. Dataset, Dryad CC0. Associated article: Journal of Thermal Biology 114: 103583, doi:10.1016/j.jtherbio.2023.103583. 

@Misc{,
  title = {Data for: Interspecific Differences in Thermal Tolerance Landscape Explain Aphid Community Abundance under Climate Change},
  author = {Y J Li and S Y Chen and L B Jorgensen and J Overgaard and D Renault and H Colinet and C S Ma},
  year = {2023},
  doi = {10.5061/dryad.mcvdnck4j},
  note = {Dataset, Dryad CC0. Associated article: Journal of Thermal Biology 114: 103583, doi:10.1016/j.jtherbio.2023.103583.},
}

Arnold P (2026). “Thermal Load Sensitivity of Photosystem II in Snow Gum.” doi:10.64898/2026.04.09.717599. Snow-gum PSII source, CC BY-NC 4.0. The freqTLS development copy is for the approved non-commercial GitHub/pkgdown teaching use; see inst/COPYRIGHTS for the unresolved CRAN/commercial redistribution gate. 

@Misc{,
  title = {Thermal Load Sensitivity of Photosystem II in Snow Gum},
  author = {Pieter A. Arnold},
  year = {2026},
  doi = {10.64898/2026.04.09.717599},
  note = {Snow-gum PSII source, CC BY-NC 4.0. The freqTLS development copy is for the approved non-commercial GitHub/pkgdown teaching use; see inst/COPYRIGHTS for the unresolved CRAN/commercial redistribution gate.},
}

Saruhashi S, Boerrigter JGJ, Hooymans MHL, Sinclair BJ, Verberk WCEP (2026). “Data and Code for: Oxygen Availability and Oxygen Delivery but Not Oxidative Stress Shape Heat Tolerance in Diploid and Triploid Zebrafish Larvae.” doi:10.5281/zenodo.20075355. Dataset, CC BY 4.0. Associated article: Journal of Experimental Biology 229(10): jeb251548, doi:10.1242/jeb.251548. 

@Misc{,
  title = {Data and Code for: Oxygen Availability and Oxygen Delivery but Not Oxidative Stress Shape Heat Tolerance in Diploid and Triploid Zebrafish Larvae},
  author = {S Saruhashi and J G J Boerrigter and M H L Hooymans and B J Sinclair and W C E P Verberk},
  year = {2026},
  doi = {10.5281/zenodo.20075355},
  note = {Dataset, CC BY 4.0. Associated article: Journal of Experimental Biology 229(10): jeb251548, doi:10.1242/jeb.251548.},
}
